taxonomy: Ditaxis claryana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Ditaxis, which comprises several species of shrubs and trees native to Central and South America. The Euphorbiaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants, commonly known as the spurge family. Members of this family are often characterized by their milky sap and umbrella-like inflorescences. The exact classification of Ditaxis claryana is further refined in botanical nomenclature systems, such as the APG III system.
